title: Product Owner
description:
**Lincotek,** headquartered in Rubbiano, Parma, Italy, is a global contract manufacturer for services in markets including Industrial Gas Turbines, Aerospace and Medical Device applications, as well as a leading manufacturer of industrial coating equipment and one of the most respected producers in the Additive Manufacturing field. The Group has more than 1,800 employees located in 18 production facilities across Europe, North America and Asia.  

  



  

**Posizione:**
--------------

### **ABOUT THE JOB**


Under the guidance of the Product Engineering Manager, the Product Owner (PO) leads the technical and production interface between internal manufacturing teams and designated OEMs for IGT or Aviation components.


The role is responsible for managing the end\-to\-end manufacturing process of assigned components, ensuring alignment on technical requirements, quality standards, delivery timelines, and cost targets. A key focus is placed on complex manufacturing processes such as coating, drilling, and heat treatment.


The PO also coordinates the Manufacturing Engineer (ME) team, ensuring the definition and execution of manufacturing cycles and operational instructions.

### **MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES:**


The PO shall perform each of the Major Activities, including, but not limited to, the following:

* Act as primary technical interface with OEMs, ensuring alignment on specifications, timing, quality, and cost.
* Lead regular meetings with OEMs to define technical requirements, production strategies, and new product introductions.
* Coordinate and supervise Manufacturing Engineers supporting OEM programs.
* Ensure alignment between Production, Quality, and Engineering on production planning and industrialization activities.
* Manage project reviews (internal and customer\-facing) and ensure timely delivery within budget.
* Analyze customer specifications and drawings, lead qualification kick\-off activities and implementation.
* Perform feasibility studies and support product industrialization, including interaction with suppliers when required.
* Define manufacturing processes, including process flows, cycle times, and BOMs, in collaboration with cross\-functional teams.
* Support Design and Production teams to ensure product manufacturability.
* Lead product and process qualification, including preparation of required documentation and change management.
* Provide engineering support to Production and Quality to prevent and resolve non\-conformities.
* Collaborate with Quality on validation of processes, equipment, and critical suppliers.

### **SUPERVISORY RESPONSIBILITY:**

Manufacturing Engineer team.

**Requisiti:**
--------------

### **EDUCATION AND/OR EXPERIENCE:**

* Master’s degree in Mechanical, Manufacturing, Aerospace, Materials Science, or related field.
* 3\+ years of experience in Engineering, Manufacturing or Quality roles.
* Experience in Industrial Gas Turbine, Aerospace, or Automotive industries preferred.
* Strong knowledge of GD\&T and manufacturing processes.
* Experience with NPI/APQP and industrialization processes.
* Familiarity with ERP systems (SAP preferred).

### **SKILLS:**

* Strong project management and problem\-solving skills.
* Ability to read and interpret technical drawings, specifications, and procedures.
* Solid understanding of manufacturing processes and quality standards.
* Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.
* Ability to work independently, manage priorities, and drive results.
* Detail\-oriented with strong analytical capabilities.
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Project).
* Fluent in Italian and English.

### **WORK ENVIRONMENT:**


This job operates in both an office environment and a manufacturing environment. The role routinely uses standard office equipment, such as computers, phones, photocopiers, filing cabinets and fax machines. The individual will be required to adhere to safety policies and procedures on the manufacturing floor.

### **PHYSICAL DEMAND:**


While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to talk or hear. The employee is frequently required to stand, walk, use hands to finger, handle or feel and reach with hands and arms.

### **POSITION TYPE/EXPECTED HOURS OF WORK:**


This is a full\-time position, Monday through Friday 8am to 5pm, with additional hours as business needs necessitate.

### **TRAVEL**


Occasional overseas and domestic travel may be required to support the rapid growth of the company.

**Altre informazioni:**
-----------------------

Lincotek provides equal employment opportunities to all individuals regardless of their race, color, creed, religion, gender, age, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by state, federal, or local law.

title: Clinical Specialist (Operating Room Experience) - Bologna / Firenze
Batch Record Reviewer
description:
**Batch Record Reviewer**

 *Be a part of a revolutionary change*

 *At PMI, we’ve chosen to do something incredible. We’re totally transforming our business and building our future on smoke‑free products with the power to deliver a smoke‑free future.*  

*With huge change comes huge opportunity. So, wherever you join us, you’ll enjoy the freedom to dream up and deliver better, brighter solutions, and the space to move your career forward in endlessly different directions.*

*PMI’s shift from selling cigarettes to our new smoke‑free products has changed the way we work. New products bring new challenges—in how we source and purchase raw materials, how we engineer and manufacture, and how we plan our supply chain.*

  

As a **Batch Record Reviewer**, you will play a key role in ensuring that smoke‑free products are manufactured, documented, and released in full compliance with Quality standards and regulatory requirements. The role sits at the intersection of **Quality, Manufacturing, and Data**, with a strong focus on accuracy, consistency, and risk prevention.


You will support production activities by reviewing and managing **Manufacturing Batch Records and Batch Release documentation**, ensuring that all data is complete, accurate, traceable, and properly archived according to PMI guidelines and the Quality Management System.


The position will be based in **Crespellano (BO)**.

 **Your Responsibilities**

 *Quality Support*


Provide Quality support and expertise to continuously improve products and Quality systems.  

Ensure timely Quality support in response to product quality issues by performing quality risk assessments and defining clear and effective action plans that immediately reduce negative impacts on Quality and Production KPIs.  

Provide on‑floor support for the implementation of Quality standards and self‑control systems, ensuring products and processes meet Quality requirements throughout the entire manufacturing line.

 *Analysis and Reporting*


Support report generation and data analysis activities as required, ensuring data compliance, accuracy, and integrity.  

Participate in and support the monitoring of key Quality KPIs.

 *Non‑Conformities and CAPA*


Establish and maintain a system to identify and segregate all non‑conforming intermediate and finished products, whether detected in the warehouse, laboratory, production area, or post‑production.  

Investigate and resolve critical deviations, define disposition actions, and implement corrective actions when required.  

Define and implement a monitoring system for manufactured products through historical data analysis and trend evaluation to identify potential deviations and preventive actions (CAPA).  

Ensure the execution, maintenance, and timely updating of Quality reports.

 *RRP Batch Record Documentation*


Support the manufacturing team in the preparation and revision of batch documentation, providing guidance on data and record management.  

Ensure Manufacturing Batch Records and Batch Release documentation are accurate, complete, reviewed, and properly archived in compliance with PMI guidelines and QMS requirements, confirming product readiness for commercial release.

 *Communication \& Cooperation*


Communicate relevant Quality‑related information effectively to cross‑functional stakeholders and support teams to ensure smooth Quality processes and the achievement of productivity and Quality objectives.  

Provide support during product and process improvement trials, as well as during new platform development activities.  

Support Quality Engineers in defining new Quality standards for new products.

 *Continuous Improvement \& Innovation*


Challenge standard processes and products to propose productivity and efficiency improvement initiatives.  

Support teams in planning and executing tests and related analyses, applying the principles of Total Quality, Lean Manufacturing, and Total Productive Maintenance (TPM).

 *Training*


Deliver internal training to manufacturing personnel as a Subject Matter Expert (SME) in Quality processes and products, with the goal of increasing overall Quality awareness and competence.

 *Health, Safety \& Environment*


Promote and comply with all PMI policies and regulations related to Quality, Environment, Health, and Safety.  

Identify and report situations requiring action to minimize or eliminate risks to personnel, company assets, and societal impact, ensuring compliance with—or anticipation of—applicable legal requirements and corporate standards.

 **Who We’re Looking For**

  

* College or University degree in Biology, Engineering, or a Scientific discipline, or equivalent skills demonstrated through relevant professional experience
* Local language: Fluent
* English: Fluent
* At least 1 year of experience in Production, Quality, or an equivalent environment
* Knowledge of relevant international standards (e.g., ISO), Quality Assurance processes, and statistical and analytical tools
* Strong ability to analyze data and translate it into meaningful information through effective reporting
* Knowledge of pharmaceutical and/or medical device industry regulations is considered an additional asset

 **Contract type:** Fixed‑term, 6‑month duration

 **What We Offer**

  

Our success depends on people who come to work every single day with a sense of purpose and an appetite for progress.


Join PMI and you can:

* Seize the freedom to define your future—and ours. We’ll empower you to take risks, experiment, and explore.
* Be part of an inclusive, diverse culture where everyone’s contribution is respected; collaborate with some of the world’s best people and feel like you belong.
* Pursue your ambitions and develop your skills with a global business—our staggering size and scale provide endless opportunities to progress.
* Take pride in delivering our promise to society: improving the lives of a billion smokers.

  

PM MTB is Philip Morris International’s *Center of Excellence*, globally recognized for prototyping, mass‑volume production, and employee training on smoke‑free products. PM MTB employs more than 1,000 people, and its Crespellano production site is one of the most advanced manufacturing facilities in Italy for technological innovation, competence development, and talent recognition.


In 2023, Philip Morris Manufacturing \& Technology Bologna was awarded *Top Employer* for the ninth year in a row. We have also been granted the *Equal Salary**Certification* by the homonymous foundation, certifying that men and women are paid equally for equivalent work.


Bring us your curiosity and your desire to challenge the everyday, and we’ll give you real responsibility. You’ll help us improve, inject innovation, and create new ways of working—solving supply‑chain problems that span the whole world: a smoke‑free world.
company_description: PMI is the leading international tobacco company with a difference. With a team of colleagues worldwide, we’re ready to have global impact and disrupt an industry, together.
